message: chore(beads): continue hgjp subtask status shuffles

@@ -188,13 +188,13 @@
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.28","title":"Accessibility pass: focus, alt, heading hierarchy, reduced motion","description":"Full a11y audit over the article once all sections and visualizations are in.

## Checklist
- [ ] Every interactive element has a visible focus ring (Tailwind `focus-visible:ring-*`).
- [ ] Heading hierarchy is correct: one `h1` (hero), then `h2` per section, `h3` per subsection. No skipped levels.
- [ ] Every `<Image>` has meaningful `alt` text; decorative elements use `alt=""`.
- [ ] Every visualization has a descriptive `aria-label` and, where appropriate, a `<figure>` + `<figcaption>` wrapper.
- [ ] All visualizations respect `prefers-reduced-motion` (no animations when set).
- [ ] Keyboard navigation works through every visualization (Tab order makes sense; arrow keys where natural).
- [ ] Color contrast ≥ 4.5:1 for body text, ≥ 3:1 for large text; ≥ 3:1 for UI state indicators (focus rings, active states).
- [ ] Skip link present (already in site layout, verify not broken).
- [ ] `aria-live` regions for the install sections "copied to clipboard" feedback (if we add that inline here).
- [ ] TOC anchors match section IDs exactly.

## Tools
- `bun lint` (eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y should catch the bulk).
- Manual keyboard walk through the whole article.
- Axe DevTools in the browser.

## Acceptance criteria
- Zero a11y warnings from eslint.
- Axe clean on the rendered page.
- Keyboard walk through the article without a mouse: reader can get to every meaningful interaction.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:42:49.495786737Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:16.354501572Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.28","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:42:49.495786737Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.28","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:16.203071126Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.28","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.25","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:16.354021373Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.29","title":"Lighthouse / Core Web Vitals check","description":"Run Lighthouse against the article on a dev build + production build.

## Targets
- **LCP** ≤ 2.5s (hero image must use `priority` + blur placeholder; already the pattern).
- **CLS** ≤ 0.1 (visualizations must reserve layout space before mount; use explicit width/height or `aspect-ratio`).
- **INP** ≤ 200ms (scroll-linked effects must be passive; visualizations must not block the main thread on mount).
- **Performance** ≥ 90.
- **Accessibility** = 100.
- **Best Practices** ≥ 95.
- **SEO** = 100.

## If targets fail
- Visualizations too heavy? Check bundle splits: each dynamic import should land in its own chunk. Consider further code-splitting (e.g., antipattern cards flip state machine).
- LCP late? Verify the hero image is in the initial HTML (not SSR-skipped), has `priority`, and uses a proper blur placeholder.
- CLS high? The usual suspect is a visualization whose height isnt reserved; add `min-h-[Npx]` to the viz wrapper.

## Acceptance criteria
- Lighthouse targets met on production build (Vercel preview deploy).
- Any failure is resolved at root cause before merge.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:43:00.532041834Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:16.798249640Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.29","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:43:00.532041834Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.29","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.28","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:16.797700271Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.3","title":"Build jargon glossary (lib/wills-estate-jargon.ts)","description":"Create `lib/wills-estate-jargon.ts` modeled exactly on `lib/slack-migration-jargon.ts`: export a `getJargon(term: string)` function that returns a `{ term, definition, ... }` record.

## Why
The article uses the `<J t="term">` component to produce dotted-underline tooltips over technical terms. Estate planning has a dense jargon layer (QTIP, GRAT, SLAT, ILIT, QDOT, DSUE, GST, QPRT, CRT, CLT, DAF, POD, TOD, RLT, POA, ERISA, Medicaid lookback, portability, step-up basis, elective share, community property, pretermitted heir, ancillary probate, Ulysses clause, etc.), and the article explicitly targets non-technical readers. Tooltips let us say "ILIT" in the text without derailing the reader.

## Term list (starter — expand as article sections go in)
- will, trust, revocable-trust, irrevocable-trust, pour-over-will, holographic-will
- POA, durable-POA, springing-POA, healthcare-proxy, living-will, HIPAA, POLST-MOLST
- beneficiary, contingent-beneficiary, per-stirpes, per-capita, portability, DSUE
- probate, intestacy, elective-share, pretermitted-heir, ancillary-probate, homestead
- QTIP, credit-shelter-trust, QDOT, ILIT, GRAT, SLAT, IDGT, QPRT, DAPT, dynasty-trust
- step-up-basis, disclaimer, §6166, 706-portability, GST-exemption
- titling, joint-tenancy, tenancy-by-entirety, community-property, TOD-deed, Lady-Bird-deed
- ERISA, 401k-spousal-consent, IRA-stretch, Secure-Act
- guardian, trustee, executor, fiduciary, co-trustee, successor-trustee
- domicile, snowbird, multi-state-probate
- asset-protection, Medicaid-lookback, SNT, ABLE, incentive-trust, Ulysses-clause
- NFA-gun-trust, crypto-seed, self-custody, legacy-contact
- charitable: DAF, CRT, CLT, private-foundation
- OBBBA, $15M-exemption, basis-dominance

## Structure per term
```ts
{
  term: "QTIP",
  full: "Qualified Terminable Interest Property trust",
  definition: "A trust for the surviving spouse where they get income for life, but the remainder goes to beneficiaries you chose (often first-marriage children). Prevents a widowed spouse from rewriting the plan and disinheriting the first marriage.",
  source: { label: "IRC §2056(b)(7)", href: "..." }  // optional
}
```

## Acceptance criteria
- File exists, ~40+ terms defined.
- Imported and used via `<J t="qtip">` inline in the article component.
- Each definition is plain-English (grandma-test: a reader who has never heard the term should understand it in one sentence).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:34:58.049538940Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:34:58.049538940Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.3","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:34:58.049538940Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.30","title":"Playwright smoke test for the wills-and-estate-planning article","description":"Add Playwright e2e test: `tests/wills-and-estate-planning.spec.ts` (or wherever existing tests live).

## Test scenarios
1. **Article renders**: visit `/writing/wills-and-estate-planning`, expect `h1` with "Drafting a Serious Estate Plan" (or final title) to be visible.
2. **TOC links work**: click each TOC link, expect the URL hash to change and the corresponding section heading to be in viewport.
3. **Install tabs switch**: on the install section, clicking each of the three tabs / cards reveals its content.
4. **Each visualization mounts** without console errors: scroll to each visualization, assert that the vizs root element has a known data-attribute (add `data-viz="tier-triage"` etc. in the viz beads for easy selection).
5. **Primer download button** produces a download request to `/wills-and-estate-planning-primer.md`.
6. **Mobile viewport** (`page.setViewportSize({ width: 375, height: 812 })`): article renders without horizontal scroll; no layout overflow.
7. **Reduced-motion**: set `page.emulateMedia({ reducedMotion: "reduce" })` and verify visualizations render statically (no animation-frame errors).

## Integration with existing test setup
Mirror `tests/slack-migration.spec.ts` (or whichever test file the slack article has). Reuse any shared helpers.

## Acceptance criteria
- `bunx playwright test tests/wills-and-estate-planning.spec.ts` passes in CI.
- Test file committed; runs in the same CI pipeline as existing tests.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:43:12.095530382Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:57:25.280270059Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.30","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:43:12.095530382Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.30","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:16.505452650Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.30","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.25","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:16.650258046Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":7,"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.30","author":"ubuntu","text":"REVIEW 2026-04-18: Enhance Playwright test with:
(a) Console-error assertions: fail the test if ANY console.error or uncaught rejection fires during the walk. This catches broken visualizations silently degrading.
(b) axe-playwright a11y scan at each section anchor (not just the page-load state). Install axe-core integration if not already present.
(c) Screenshot-on-failure with full-page captures stored as CI artifacts named <test>-<step>.png.
(d) Trace/video recording enabled for failed runs (already standard in Playwright's retain-on-failure config; verify it is enabled).
(e) Structured test logs: every test step logged with scenario name, step description, and outcome. Use the colorized reporter so CI output is readable.
(f) Each visualization's data-viz marker checked before AND after reduced-motion emulation.
(g) A 'primer download' test: assert the response content-type is text/markdown (or application/octet-stream) and the body is not empty.","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:57:25Z"}]}
-{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.31","title":"Final build + commit + push (article-side)","description":"Final gate before the article goes live.

## Sequence
1. `bun run build` — zero errors.
2. `bun tsc --noEmit` — clean.
3. `bun lint` — clean.
4. `ubs <changed-files>` — exit 0.
5. `bunx playwright test` — existing suites + new wills-article smoke test all pass.
6. Visual QA on the Vercel preview deploy (one human pass through the article at desktop + mobile viewports).
7. `br sync --flush-only` then `git add .beads/ content/ components/ app/writing/wills-and-estate-planning/ lib/ public/ assets/ && git commit` with a message like: "feat(writing): launch wills-and-estate-planning article with 5 visualizations + gated agent-install flow".
8. Push to `main` (Vercel auto-deploys).
9. Verify production URL renders correctly.
10. Verify OG card renders when the URL is pasted into Twitter/X, Slack, LinkedIn.

## Acceptance criteria
- Article live at https://jeffreyemanuel.com/writing/wills-and-estate-planning.
- OG card renders correctly on at least Twitter/X and Slack.
- No production console errors.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:43:21.453768573Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:51:16.439963162Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.31","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:43:21.453768573Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.31","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.26","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:17.263462067Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.31","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.27","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:17.568096862Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.31","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.29","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:16.949387368Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.31","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.30","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:17.105540170Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.31","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.32","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:51:16.439365552Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.31","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:17.418534035Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}

errors.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:43:21.453768573Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T23:03:06.927724506Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.31","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:43:21.453768573Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.31","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.26","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:17.263462067Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.31","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.27","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:17.568096862Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.31","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.29","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:16.949387368Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.31","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.30","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:17.105540170Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.31","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.32","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:51:16.439365552Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.31","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.34","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:03:06.341447721Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.31","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:03:06.693622223Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.31","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_si  {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.32","title":"Cross-repo coordination note: article install section ↔ jsm.md install UI","description":"# Cross-repo coordination note

## The dependency that crosses repo boundaries
- `jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.11` (article install section) references live URLs on jeffreys-skills.md: the skill detail page, the "Generate install prompt" button, etc.
- `bd-3t7zd.19` (jsm.md production deploy) is what makes those URLs real.

`br` does not natively track cross-repo dependencies. This bead exists so that whoever is working on the article remembers to either:
1. **Ship the article AFTER the jsm.md production deploy lands** — simplest, and the URLs in the install section will work on day one.
2. **Ship the article first with "coming soon" framing in the install section** — and file a follow-up bead to revise the article the day jsm.md goes live. Acceptable only if the jsm.md timeline slips.

## Coordination checklist (before pushing the article)
- [ ] `bd-3t7zd.19` (jsm.md prod deploy) is closed OR the articles install section is temporarily framed as "coming soon this week."
- [ ] The skill page URL in the article resolves: `curl -I https://jeffreys-skills.md/skills/wills-and-estate-planning-skill` returns 200 (or 302 → login; either is fine).
- [ ] The install-card on jsm.md is live and functional end-to-end — one of us has successfully installed the skill via the paste flow at least once on staging or prod.

## Acceptance criteria
- This bead stays open until the article ships with working (not placeholder) install URLs.
- Close with a comment noting which of the two framings was used at ship time.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:51:12.241720160Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:51:12.241720160Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.32","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:51:12.241720160Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.33","title":"Error boundary around dynamic-imported visualizations in the article","description":"# Error boundary for visualizations

The article uses Next dynamic({ ssr: false }) to load five visualizations (TierTriageViz, AxiomCoherenceViz, IntakePhasesViz, DeliverablesTreeViz, AntiPatternCardsViz). Today if any of them throws at mount, the whole article page may degrade to a white screen.

## Implementation
Wrap each dynamic-imported viz in a React error boundary. Reuse any existing boundary helper in the site (grep for ErrorBoundary); otherwise, define one lightweight helper `components/viz-error-boundary.tsx` with:

- `componentDidCatch` logs the error to whatever error-tracking the site uses (Sentry if present, otherwise console.error with a stable prefix `[wep-viz-error]`).
- Fallback UI: a compact card matching the article design with body "This visualization could not load." and a "Retry" button that re-mounts the wrapped component. Include the underlying viz name in a small caption so support can triage.
- Respect prefers-reduced-motion on the fallback.
- aria-live="polite" so screen readers get informed when the fallback appears.

## Tests
Playwright scenario: force one viz to throw via a test-only `?break=tier-triage` query param recognized by the viz scaffold bead (hgjp.20). Assert:
- The rest of the article still renders.
- The fallback card appears where the broken viz was.
- Clicking Retry actually re-mounts the viz (send a second `?break=` that disables break-mode on the retry).
- Error logged to console with the expected prefix.

## Acceptance criteria
- Every dynamic-imported viz wrapped.
- Playwright test covers the forced-break scenario.
- No regression in happy-path viz render.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:59:23.754267463Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:59:23.754267463Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.33","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:59:23.754267463Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.34","title":"Content QA + proofreading pass before publish","description":"# Content QA + proofreading pass

Explicit human-review gate before the article goes live. The article is launching a paid product to a non-technical audience; typos and confusing copy directly translate to lost subscriptions.

## Review checklist
- [ ] Read the article end-to-end once on desktop, once on mobile.
- [ ] Every claim about the skill ("17 subagents", "45 deliverables", "8 operating modes", "9 intake phases", "11 axioms", "14 operators", "135 reference files", ~$15M 2026 exemption, OBBBA reference, etc.) is factually correct against the current SKILL.md.
- [ ] Every URL to jsm.md resolves — no 404s.
- [ ] Every `<J>` jargon tooltip renders a plain-English definition; no "?? undefined" in prod.
- [ ] Every section heading anchor (`#install`, `#who`, `#insight`, etc.) links from the TOC correctly.
- [ ] No typos, inconsistent capitalization, or duplicated sentences. Run the article copy through a proofing pass.
- [ ] Disclaimers (not-legal-advice, attorney-handoff) are present and prominent.
- [ ] No implied guarantees ("you will save $X") — frame as "readers have saved between $X and $Y" with the X-post framing.
- [ ] Author byline + publish date visible.
- [ ] OG card renders correctly when pasted into Twitter/X, Slack, LinkedIn, iMessage.
- [ ] Read the primer markdown (hgjp.6) in full — is it internally consistent? Does it pair with the article content?
- [ ] Walk one non-technical friend through the article (not a developer) and observe where they stumble — log each stumble as a follow-up bead.

## Outcome
A short "proof of review" comment on this bead noting who reviewed, what they fixed, and any follow-ups filed.

## Acceptance criteria
- Article passes the end-to-end read aloud without the reviewer flinching.
- At least one non-dev has walked through the install section and successfully pasted the prompt into Claude/Codex (can pair with bd-3t7zd.18 staging verify).
- No open P1 typo/copy bugs on the article.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:59:40.864491627Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:59:40.864491627Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.34","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:59:40.864491627Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.35","title":"Sitemap + RSS + social-preview entries for the new article","description":"# Sitemap + RSS update

## Checks
- [ ] `/sitemap.xml` (check `app/sitemap.ts` or equivalent) includes `/writing/wills-and-estate-planning` with the right `lastmod`.
- [ ] `/robots.txt` does not disallow the new path.
- [ ] RSS feed (if the site has one — grep for `/rss`, `/feed`, `app/feed.xml/route.ts`) includes the new article.
- [ ] Writing-index page (`/writing`) surfaces the article card.
- [ ] `manifest.ts` + favicon behave correctly on the new route.

## Verification
- `curl -s https://jeffreyemanuel.com/sitemap.xml | grep wills-and-estate-planning` returns a hit.
- Paste URL into https://cards-dev.twitter.com/validator (or the current Twitter/X card validator) and confirm the OG card renders.
- Paste into https://www.linkedin.com/post-inspector/ and verify.

## Acceptance criteria
- Sitemap entry present.
- RSS entry present (if RSS exists on the site).
- Social-preview validators return the expected card without warnings.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:59:50.222423103Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:59:50.222423103Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.35","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:59:50.222423103Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.36","title":"Analytics: conversion events on article CTAs","description":"# Analytics events on article CTAs

Knowing which part of the article converts is how we iterate. Zero analytics = flying blind.

## Events to emit
Use whatever analytics the site already has (check `components/` for existing analytics wrappers; probably GA4 or Plausible). Emit:
- `article_viewed` (slug=wills-and-estate-planning, scroll_depth_bucket on unload: 0-25/25-50/50-75/75-100)
- `primer_download_clicked` (from the download CTA section)
- `install_section_cta_clicked` (target: "claude" | "codex" | "cli" | "manual-zip" — depending on which card the user clicks)
- `jsm_skill_page_outbound_clicked` (target: "wills-and-estate-planning-skill" or whichever URL was clicked)
- `toc_link_clicked` (anchor id)
- `visualization_interacted` (viz_name, action: "tier_selected" | "axiom_toggled" | "phase_drilled" | "deliverable_opened" | "antipattern_flipped")

All events include: referrer, session_id, article_slug, viewport_class ("mobile" | "tablet" | "desktop"). PII-free — no user_id (the article is public, so there IS no authenticated user on this page).

## Implementation
Define a tiny `emitEvent(name, props)` helper in `lib/analytics.ts` (or extend existing) if not already present. The article component calls it on the listed interactions.

## Privacy
Honour Do-Not-Track + the sites cookie-consent posture. If the site already has an analytics layer with opt-out wiring, inherit it automatically.

## Acceptance criteria
- Events observable in the analytics dashboard within 24h of deploy.
- No PII in any event.
- Dev-mode (NODE_ENV=development) can log events to console instead of sending, for debugging.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:00:04.316751104Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T23:00:04.316751104Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.36","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:00:04.316751104Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.33","title":"Error boundary around dynamic-imported visualizations in the article","description":"# Error boundary for visualizations

The article uses Next dynamic({ ssr: false }) to load five visualizations (TierTriageViz, AxiomCoherenceViz, IntakePhasesViz, DeliverablesTreeViz, AntiPatternCardsViz). Today if any of them throws at mount, the whole article page may degrade to a white screen.

## Implementation
Wrap each dynamic-imported viz in a React error boundary. Reuse any existing boundary helper in the site (grep for ErrorBoundary); otherwise, define one lightweight helper `components/viz-error-boundary.tsx` with:

- `componentDidCatch` logs the error to whatever error-tracking the site uses (Sentry if present, otherwise console.error with a stable prefix `[wep-viz-error]`).
- Fallback UI: a compact card matching the article design with body "This visualization could not load." and a "Retry" button that re-mounts the wrapped component. Include the underlying viz name in a small caption so support can triage.
- Respect prefers-reduced-motion on the fallback.
- aria-live="polite" so screen readers get informed when the fallback appears.

## Tests
Playwright scenario: force one viz to throw via a test-only `?break=tier-triage` query param recognized by the viz scaffold bead (hgjp.20). Assert:
- The rest of the article still renders.
- The fallback card appears where the broken viz was.
- Clicking Retry actually re-mounts the viz (send a second `?break=` that disables break-mode on the retry).
- Error logged to console with the expected prefix.

## Acceptance criteria
- Every dynamic-imported viz wrapped.
- Playwright test covers the forced-break scenario.
- No regression in happy-path viz render.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:59:23.754267463Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T23:03:04.468477393Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.33","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:59:23.754267463Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.33","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:03:04.467669089Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","title":"Scaffold article component (components/wills-estate-article.tsx)","description":"Create `components/wills-estate-article.tsx` as a "use client" component modeled exactly on `components/slack-migration-article.tsx`.

## Scope of this bead (scaffold only — sections land in follow-up beads)
- Imports: React, Next `Image`, Next fonts (Crimson_Pro, JetBrains_Mono, Bricolage_Grotesque), lucide-react icons (tentative: FileDown, ScrollText, ShieldCheck, HeartPulse, HandCoins, Landmark, Sparkles, BookOpen), `MathTooltip`, `getJargon` from `@/lib/wills-estate-jargon`, `getScrollMetrics` from `@/lib/utils`, and illustration asset.
- Dynamic imports for the five visualizations (TierTriageViz, AxiomCoherenceViz, IntakePhasesViz, DeliverablesTreeViz, AntiPatternCardsViz) from `./wills-estate-visualizations`, each `{ ssr: false }` because they use browser APIs / framer-motion.
- Font variables set up the same way as slack article (`--font-crimson`, etc).
- Wrapper components defined locally (these can be copy-pasted from slack article with the `sm-` → `wep-` class-name rename if any styles diverge; otherwise keep `sm-` so we share the stylesheet): `J` (jargon tooltip), `EC` (editorial container, 800px centered), `Divider`, `Code`, `Mono`, `SectionHeader`, `Sub`, `RefTable`.
- Primer download button constant: `PRIMER_HREF = "/wills-and-estate-planning-primer.md"`, `PRIMER_FILENAME = "WILLS_AND_ESTATE_PLANNING_PRIMER.md"`.
- Main exported component `WillsEstateArticle` with:
  - `scrollProgress` state + scroll listener (copy pattern from slack).
  - IntersectionObserver-driven section reveal (copy pattern from slack, reuses `sm-fade-section` / `sm-visible` classes in globals.css).
  - Outer `div` with ref, role="main", className combines `sm-scope sm-body` + font variable classes, same background `#020204` and text `#f8fafc`.
  - Scroll progress bar rendered at top.
  - Empty section placeholders (stubs with TODO comments) for each section bead to fill in: hero, download-cta, intro, catalog, install, who-this-is-for, core-insight, sample-session, produces, workflow, anti-patterns, verification, tldr.

## Why a single scaffold bead
Every content-section bead will modify this file. Scaffolding it once means downstream section beads can land in parallel without file-creation conflicts. Per AGENTS.md "Backwards Compatibility & File Sprawl" rule: one article component, not ten.

## Acceptance criteria
- File exists, `bun tsc --noEmit` is clean once the route + jargon beads land too.
- Has placeholder stubs for every section.
- Uses the existing `sm-*` CSS classes until we confirm we need a `wep-*` namespace (decided in the styles bead).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:35:20.549272285Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:43:55.142615976Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:35:20.549272285Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:43:55.142041930Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.5","title":"Register article in lib/content.ts + command palette","description":"Two small registrations so the article surfaces in the sites navigation:

## 1. `lib/content.ts`
Add an entry to whichever array drives the writing listing (likely `writingPosts` / `writing` / similar; grep for where slack-mattermost-migration is registered). Include:
- slug: `wills-and-estate-planning`
- title: final article title
- description: ~140 char hook suitable for the writing index card
- date: publication date
- tags: `["ai", "skills", "estate-planning", "wills", "life-planning"]`
- href: `/writing/wills-and-estate-planning`
- hero image reference

## 2. `components/command-palette.tsx` (Cmd+K search)
Add to the navigation/search array so `Cmd+K` → "wills" finds the article. Pattern: same as how the slack-mattermost article is registered.

## Why this bead is separate
These are tiny mechanical edits in existing registry files. Splitting from the article component bead avoids merge conflict with content sections and keeps the article discoverable the moment it goes live.

## Acceptance criteria
- Article shows up on `/writing`.
- `Cmd+K` → "wills" / "estate" / "planning" surfaces the article.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:35:31.551381415Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:15.911166538Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.5","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:35:31.551381415Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.5","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:15.910594517Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.6","title":"Write agent-readable primer markdown (public/wills-and-estate-planning-primer.md)","description":"Write `public/wills-and-estate-planning-primer.md` — a single-file primer that a paying subscriber can download from the article and paste into any fresh Claude / Codex / ChatGPT session to boot the agent into the wills-estate-planning mindset.

## Purpose
This is the offline-friendly equivalent of the skill itself. The full skill is 201 files, ~24k lines, ~1 MB — too big to paste into a context window. The primer is a lossy but coherent summary the agent can use even without the full skill installed. Acts as:

1. **Proof-of-value tease** for visitors who are not yet paying subscribers (they can see the shape of the skill).
2. **Emergency fallback** for a paying subscriber whose agent setup isnt cooperating — they can paste this and still get useful output.
3. **The thing the "Download the operator primer as Markdown" button on the article points at** (see `PRIMER_HREF` in the article scaffold bead).

## Structure (draws from SKILL.md at ~/jeffreys_skills/wills-and-estate-planning-skill/)
- `# Wills & Estate Planning — Operator Primer`
- TL;DR: what estate planning really is (coordination, not drafting), the 11 axioms condensed to the 5 most important ones, and the 2026 OBBBA $15M exemption reality.
- The kernel: axioms 0-11 in one-liner form with a sentence of why each matters.
- Wealth-tier routing (Tier 1-5 with one sentence each).
- The 9 intake phases as a numbered list.
- The 9-step workflow as a numbered list.
- The top 15 anti-patterns.
- The verification-first discipline (live-law checks).
- Output contract summary (intake/, analyses/, deliverables/).
- A short disclaimer: this primer is educational; an attorney signs the documents.
- Footer: "To get the full skill with all 17 subagents, 45 deliverable templates, and 135 reference files, subscribe at https://jeffreys-skills.md/ and install via the instructions at https://jeffreys-skills.md/skills/wills-and-estate-planning-skill"

## Format
- Plain Markdown.
- ~8-12 KB (smaller than slack primers 185 KB — this is deliberately a condensed version, not a complete operator guide).
- Semantic headings so an agent can index it.

## Acceptance criteria
- File exists at `public/wills-and-estate-planning-primer.md`.
- Articles download button successfully serves it as `application/octet-stream` with the configured filename.
- Content is self-contained: an agent with just this primer and no skill installed can still conduct a useful basic estate-planning intake.","notes":"test","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:35:51.386730372Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:56:46.574930540Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.6","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:35:51.386730372Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":1,"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.6","author":"ubuntu","text":"test comment","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:56:38Z"},{"id":2,"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.6","author":"ubuntu","text":"REVIEW 2026-04-18: Primer size target corrected from 8-12 KB to 30-60 KB (hard cap 200 KB). This is the single highest-leverage marketing asset on the whole article. Trim state-specific appendices (volatile anyway) before trimming axioms/operators.","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:56:46Z"}]}
